mathml_representations_list: <mstyle id="custom-group-4" class="custom-group"><mi id="mi_130">d</mi><mo stretchy="false">(</mo><mo>∂</mo><mi mathvariant="normal" id="mi_131">Ω</mi></mstyle>

concepts_list.name: u_field
concepts_list.description: Velocity field of the fluid in the domain.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'u' appears multiple times in bold, associated with terms like 'u · ∇u', '∇u', and 'u(t)', and is central to the momentum and continuity equations. It is consistently referred to as the fluid velocity in the context of conservation laws.

concepts_list.name: w_test
concepts_list.description: Test function used in the variational formulation to enforce weak form of the governing equations.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'w' is repeatedly used in the weak form of the momentum equation as a test function, appearing in dot products with velocity and stress terms. It is defined in a Sobolev space and vanishes at Dirichlet boundaries.

concepts_list.name: rho_density
concepts_list.description: Density of the fluid, appearing in both momentum and continuity equations.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'ρ' appears in multiple equations, multiplying both the time derivative of velocity and the body force term, indicating its role as a fluid density in the conservation equations.

concepts_list.name: b_body_force
concepts_list.description: Body force vector, such as gravity, acting on the fluid.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'b' appears in the momentum equation as a bold vector term, multiplied by density and dotted with the test function 'w'. It is explicitly part of the right-hand side of the momentum equation.

concepts_list.name: p_pressure
concepts_list.description: Pressure field in the fluid, used in the stress tensor and boundary integral.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'p' appears in the weak form as a scalar field, multiplied by the gradient of the test function and integrated over the domain, and is part of the stress tensor via the constitutive equation.

concepts_list.name: mu_viscosity
concepts_list.description: Dynamic viscosity of the Newtonian fluid, appearing in the stress tensor and dissipation term.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'μ' is used in the constitutive equation for the stress tensor, specifically in the term involving the symmetric gradient of velocity, indicating its role as the fluid's viscosity.

concepts_list.name: Omega_domain
concepts_list.description: The main computational domain representing the fluid region in the 3D space.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'Ω' is used to denote the fluid domain in multiple equations, including in integrals over the domain and its boundary. It is explicitly defined as the region where the fluid exists and where conservation laws are applied.

concepts_list.name: delta_Omega_boundary
concepts_list.description: The boundary of the fluid domain, where boundary conditions (Dirichlet, Neumann) are applied.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol '∂Ω' (denoted as 'delta_Omega') appears in the boundary integral term and in the description of boundary conditions. It is explicitly referenced as the boundary of the domain Ω where traction and velocity conditions are defined.

concepts_list.name: t_time
concepts_list.description: Time variable used in partial derivatives and initial conditions.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 't' appears in partial derivatives (e.g., ∂u/∂t) and in initial and boundary conditions, indicating its role as a time variable in the evolution of the fluid state.

concepts_list.name: d_Omega_differential
concepts_list.description: Differential element used in volume integrals over the domain Ω.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'dΩ' appears in multiple integrals over the domain Ω, such as ∫_Ω ρu·w dΩ, indicating its role as the volume differential in the weak formulation.

concepts_list.name: d_delta_Omega_differential
concepts_list.description: Differential element used in surface integrals over the boundary ∂Ω.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'd(∂Ω)' appears in the boundary integral term, indicating its role as the surface differential in the weak form of the momentum equation.

concepts_list.name: T_traction
concepts_list.description: Traction vector on the boundary, defined as the normal component of the stress tensor.
concepts_list.justification: The symbol 'T' appears as a bold vector in the boundary condition and in the constitutive equation as T = n · σ, indicating its role as the traction vector on the boundary.

The symbol 'u' appears multiple times in bold, associated with terms like 'u · ∇u', '∇u', and 'u(t)', and is central to the momentum and continuity equations. It is consistently referred to as the fluid velocity in the context of conservation laws.
